pages.title: About John - John Healey MP
pages.meta_description:
John is the Labour Member of Parliament for Rawmarsh and Conisbrough in South Yorkshire and is Secretary of State for Defence.
He has previously held the following positions in the Shadow Cabinet and Labour Government:

Shadow Secretary of State for Housing
Shadow Secretary of State for Health
Minister for Housing and Planning
Minister for Local Government
Minister for Floods Recovery
pages.text_excerpt: About John - John Healey MP Skip to content Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place About John You are here: Home About John About John 2025-02-27T13:55:10+00:00 John is the Labour Member of Parliament for Rawmarsh and Conisbrough in South Yorkshire and is Secretary of State for Defence. He has previously held the following positions in the Shadow Cabinet and Labour Government: Shadow Secretary of State for Housing Shadow Secretary of State for Health Minister for Housing and Planning Minister for Local Government Minister for Floods Recovery (2007) Financial Secretary to the Treasury Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for Adult Skills He has also been a member of the Education and Employment Select Committee and Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Chancellor of the Exchequer. John was elected MP for Wentworth in 1997. Boundaries changed at the 2010 general election and he became the MP for Wentworth and Dearne. Boundaries changed again at the 2024 general election and he became the MP for the newly created Rawmarsh and Conisbrough constituency. John was born in Wakefield and educated at Lady Lumley’s Comprehensive School, Pickering, and St Peter’s School in York and Christ’s College, Cambridge. He worked in the voluntary sector from 1984 to 1990 with the Royal National Institute for the Deaf, the Royal Association for Disability and Rehabilitation and MIND campaigning to improve rights and services for disabled people. From 1990 to 1994 John worked in communications for Issue Communications, a PR company, and the MSF Union. He was Campaigns Director for the TUC from 1994 to 1997. He has also been a part-time tutor at the Open University’s Business School. John is married with one son and lives in Rotherham. pages.title: Help & Advice - John Healey MP
pages.meta_description:
What do MPs do?

I am one of 650 MPs elected to represent their constituents’ interests in Parliament.

We scrutinise the government and are responsible for making and repealing laws. We also debate issues. You can visit Parliament’s website for more information.
pages.text_excerpt: Help & Advice - John Healey MP Skip to content Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Help & Advice You are here: Home Help & Advice Help & Advice 2024-11-26T13:53:16+00:00 What do MPs do? I am one of 650 MPs elected to represent their constituents’ interests in Parliament. We scrutinise the government and are responsible for making and repealing laws. We also debate issues. You can visit Parliament’s website for more information. These pages may be particularly useful to young people or schools. Some MPs are also ministers or shadow ministers and have responsibilities within a Government department or in Opposition. What can my MP help with? You can contact me with problems or concerns connected with Parliamentary or central Government matters. This could be issues linked to government departments, like the Department of Health, tax or benefit problems or queries or concerns about government policy or legislation. I may be able to help with your problem or find out information for you by contacting a Government minister on your behalf. If your issue or concern relates to services provided by your local council – such as housing, schools or waste collection – it is best to contact your local councillor. Find out more about Rotherham councillors and Doncaster Councillors > Why should I vote? People choose their MPs by voting in general elections. These elections are crucial because they decide who will be in government and give everyone a say in how the country will be run in the future. The party with the most MPs forms the government. Elections work best when everyone takes part and votes. If you don’t vote, you are having no say in issues as important as the NHS, schools, how much tax you pay and how we look after the environment. How do I register to vote? Almost everyone in Britain aged 18 and over is qualified to vote. However, you must register before you can do so. Register to vote in Rotherham and Doncaster Tours of Parliament I can organise visits to Parliament for schools, groups and individuals who live in the Rawmarsh and Conisbrough constituency – just contact my office. Please note tours are not available on Thursdays. pages.title: Get In Touch - John Healey MP
pages.meta_description:
Every year thousands of people contact me with individual issues through my constituency office in Wath-upon-Dearne, which is open full time, or by speaking to me at one of the regular advice surgeries and coffee mornings I hold around the constituency.

People ask me for assistance with local community problems, tax credits, pensions, benefits, matters involving
headings: h2: John’s Team
h2: Privacy Policy
pages.text_excerpt: Get In Touch - John Healey MP Skip to content Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Search for: Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Home Information About John Help & Advice Get In Touch News Campaigns Defence Pride in Place Get In Touch You are here: Home Get In Touch Get In Touch 2025-11-18T15:26:36+00:00 Every year thousands of people contact me with individual issues through my constituency office in Wath-upon-Dearne, which is open full time, or by speaking to me at one of the regular advice surgeries and coffee mornings I hold around the constituency. People ask me for assistance with local community problems, tax credits, pensions, benefits, matters involving the Child Support Agency and government policies and legislation, and many more issues. For more information on what MPs can and cannot do, visit the ‘Help and advice ’ page. I am always ready to listen and will do my best to help – so please get in touch should you need me at any time by completing the form below. Parliamentary rules mean I can only take up your case if you live in the Rawmarsh and Conisbrough constituency. If you’re not sure, you can check at this website . You can email me at john.healey.mp@parliament.uk . Please write or call in to: John Healey MP 79 High Street Wath Upon Dearne Rotherham S63 7QB Constituency office phone number: 01709 875943 For Ministry of Defence queries, please call 0207 218 9000 Please do not call the constituency office with defence related queries. John’s Team Holly Williamson – Head of Constituency Phone: 01709 512463 Email: holly.williamson@parliament.uk ______________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Greg Kuczmaida – Diary Manager & Caseworker Phone: 01709 875943 Email: greg.kuczmaida@parliament.uk ______________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Emily Walker – Communications Officer (Constituency) Phone: 01709 512463 Email: emily.walker@parliament.uk Nieve Elliott – Caseworker Phone: 01709 875943 Email: nieve.elliott@parliament.uk ______________________________________________________________________________________________________________ John Williams – Caseworker (part-time) Phone: 01709 875943 Email: johnb.williams@parliament.uk ______________________________________________________________________________________________________________ Jackie Bate – Office Manager Phone: 0207 219 5170 Email: batej@parliament.uk Privacy Policy I am the MP for Rawmarsh and Conisbrough Constituency. As your MP, it is important that I and my office can keep in touch with constituents about my work, take up casework on their behalf and ask for views on local issues. As a Labour MP I also have access to other information which I or my office, or volunteers working with me, will use for campaigning purposes or for communicating with Labour Party members. In respect of that information, the Labour Party is the data controller, and you should consult their privacy policy (https://labour.org.uk/privacy-policy/ ) for details of what information they hold, why, and how they use and process that information. This page explains how I collect and use personal data, the legal basis for doing so and provides information about your rights in respect of your personal data for which I am the data controller. My responsible officer for data protection can be contacted at emily.walker@parliament.uk or on 01709 875943. Data collection and use The office of John Healey MP will only collect and use personal information for the specific purpose for which it has been obtained. I will undertake casework using personal information provided by or on behalf of a constituent. It may be necessary for me to share your information with third party organisations. I will only do where it is necessary and reasonable to do
